findings.message: Untrusted user input in `importlib.import_module()` function allows an attacker to load arbitrary code. Avoid dynamic values in `importlib.import_module()` or use a whitelist to prevent running untrus

'mark_safe()' is used to mark a string as "safe" for HTML output. This disables escaping and could therefore subject the content to XSS attacks. Use 'django.utils.html.format_html()' to build HTML for

Data from request (redirect_url) is passed to redirect(). This is an open redirect and could be exploited. Ensure you are redirecting to safe URLs by using django.utils.http.is_safe_url(). See https:/

Translated strings will not be escaped when rendered in a template. This leads to a vulnerability where translators could include malicious script tags in their translations. Consider using `force_esc

Detected a template block where autoescaping is explicitly disabled with '{% autoescape off %}'. This allows rendering of raw HTML in this segment. Turn autoescaping on to prevent cross-site scripting
